properties.otherAdvice: The road has been reduced to a single lane in each direction 24/7 until approximately September 2026 for work associated with the M6 Stage 1 project.

Changes to traffic conditions will be in place on the Sydney Harbour Bridge at various times while the bridge and other infrastructure are being upgraded and maintained. These changes include a combination of lane closures and speed reductions.

At least one lane will be maintained in each direction during work hours.

Access to Cahill Exp over Circular Quay southbound may not be available at times. Motorists can detour via the city using Grosvenor St, Bridge St, and Macquarie St for access to the Eastern Suburbs and airport.
properties.displayName: CHANGED TRAFFIC CONDITIONS Sydney Harbour Bridge

properties.otherAdvice: Snowy 2.0 construction vehicles only will be allowed access.

properties.otherAdvice:
Special event clearways will be in place continuously from 2am Sat 16 May to 2am Mon 18 May at the following locations to facilitate rail replacement bus operations for Metro North West trackwork:

  • Railway St, southbound between Day St & Help St,
  • Railway St, northbound between Brown St & Help St
  • Brown St, westbound between Railway St & Pacific Hwy.

No-stopping zones will also be in place northbound on Railway St between Brown St and Help St during this time.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and businesses with parking permits or TfNSW Mobility Scheme permit holders. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
properties.displayName: SPECIAL EVENT CLEARWAYS Metro North West Trackwork

A reduced load limit is in place on Hampden Bridge.

Heavy vehicles over 23 tonnes are restricted from using the bridge and will need to plan a suitable detour.

All other vehicles will not be affected and can continue to use the bridge.

Emergency services vehicles over 23 tonnes will still be able to use the bridge at all times.

Heavy Vehicle operators of Class 1,2, and 3 vehicles travelling under notice must consult their relevant enforceable network map to confirm a suitable and approved detour route.
The ferry has a reduced capacity of 15 tonnes due to low tides

Operators of Class 1, 2 & 3 vehicles travelling under a Permit must not travel off the approved route listed in the permit unless an updated permit is obtained from the NHVR.

properties.headline: EMERGENCY ROADWORK Road damage MOUNT VICTORIA Great Western Highway at Victoria Pass
properties.otherAdvice: Great Western Hwy is open between Lithgow and Little Hartley. The highway is closed between Coxs River Rd and Victoria St.
properties.diversions:
Eastbound: Chifley Road and Darling Causeway.

Westbound: Harley Ave, Darling Causeway, Chifley Road.

This will add approximately 25 minutes to your travel time.

Through traffic on Great Western Hwy can consider alternative routes such as the Golden Hwy or Hume Hwy.

  • Detour is suitable for general access vehicles and Performance Based Standards (PBS) Level 1 heavy vehicles up to 20 metres long
  • Restricted access heavy vehicle operators who are unable to travel on an approved Notice Network are advised to reroute their journeys.
  • Restricted access heavy vehicle operators who are unable to travel on a Notice Network are advised to apply for an amended permit via NHVR Go. 
  • Please note that Class 1 Oversize and/or Over Mass (OSOM) vehicles and Special Purpose Vehicles (SPV) cannot currently be accommodated on the Chifley Rd/Darling Causeway detour route. 
  • To check approved Notice Networks, heavy vehicle operators can use the NHVR National Network Map to find an alternative route for their combination: https://www.nhvr.gov.au/road-access/route-planner/national-network-map

Operators who require a NHVR issued Permit are reminded that they must only travel on the roads approved in their Permit.

properties.otherAdvice:
From 6am Sat 16 May 2026:

Alfred St North between Whaling Rd and Mount St:

  • The left turn from Mount St eastbound into Alfred St North northbound will reopen.
  • Alfred St North southbound between Kurraba Rd and Mount St will also reopen. 
  • 43 public parking bays will be provided as per the end-state design.
Motorists are advised to expect intermittent closures.

properties.otherAdvice:
The speed limit on the Cahill Expressway Circular Quay Overpass will be reduced to 40km/h from 6pm to 11pm nightly from Friday 22 May until Saturday 13 June for driver distraction safety during Vivid Sydney.

During drone and laser shows in Darling Harbour the speed limit on the Western Distributor from Pyrmont to the city may also be reduced to 40km/h.

For details on road closures and parking restrictions during Vivid, see the detailed entry here.
Road closure lines provided by Google Maps may not be up to date.
